label: Vol Surface Dispersion Rotation
thesis: We believe we can create alpha by rotating among equity, duration, and defensive sleeves based on the cross-sectional shape and disagreement of listed equity volatility surfaces rather than on raw VIX level alone, capturing regime transitions earlier when index-implied stress and single-name dispersion decouple.
explainer: This family is distinct from simple VIX threshold allocation because it uses a local, bounded state vector derived from option-implied information: index term structure, skew, and cross-sectional single-name implied volatility dispersion relative to index volatility. The core hypothesis is that risk regimes are not well described by spot VIX alone. When index vol rises with shallow single-name dispersion, the market often reflects macro hedging demand and can mean-revert; when single-name dispersion and downside skew rise together, stress is more likely to propagate and equity beta should be reduced more aggressively. A paper-only implementation can classify daily regimes for SPY, QQQ, TLT, and GLD using option-chain-derived features from supported equity-options datasets, then test allocation policies such as equity overweight, barbell defense, or duration-plus-gold defense. The family remains incubatable because it is daily frequency, uses a small instrument set, and can be evaluated with end-of-day historical chains and ETF prices before any runtime consideration.
incubation_notes: Use daily end-of-day option chains on SPY and QQQ to build features: front-back term slope, 25-delta put skew proxy, and cross-sectional implied volatility dispersion from a fixed basket of liquid constituents or sector ETFs.
Start with paper-only allocations across SPY, QQQ, TLT, and GLD; avoid shorting in initial incubation to keep the family bounded and easier to validate.
Benchmark against the original VIX-threshold strategy to prove incremental explanatory power from surface features rather than level effects.
Constrain regime definitions to 3-4 discrete states learned from rolling historical clustering or fixed feature thresholds to reduce overfitting.
Run robustness checks across calm, inflation, and crisis subperiods, with turnover, gap risk, and allocation stability diagnostics.
If single-name option coverage is sparse, a fallback version can approximate dispersion using sector ETF implied vol spread versus index implied vol while preserving the family thesis.
Research tier deep_review should prioritize feature stationarity, data quality around option expiries, and sensitivity to chain selection rules.
